title: Why do most AI trading platforms ignore the compliance question?
author: comfort_fi
selftext:
Something about this has been bothering me. You see platforms brag about Sharpe ratios and fancy backtests, but the moment you ask about audit trails or regulatory alignment, things get strangely quiet.  
  
And honestly, these are the questions that matter. If an AI driven recommendation goes wrong, who’s accountable? What record exists of how the decision was made? Can the system even be explained in a way regulators accept?  
  
These aren’t theoretical issues anymore. The sec is tightening oversight. mifid II pushes for explainability. Other regions are building frameworks too. The whole environment is shifting.  
  
I’ve been keeping an eye on how different companies are responding. One example is waton financial, which is nasdaq listed and working on something called mota. What stood out wasn’t the tech but the fact that they seem to be building the regulatory infrastructure before the trading features. Kind of the opposite of how most platforms move.  
  
No idea if that approach will pay off, but it does make me think. As the rules get stricter, the platforms that ignored compliance may hit walls fast.  
  
I'm curious how others see this evolving.
